root_cause.text: The password reset link's underlying expiring code was only consumed upon the actual password reset form submission (POST), meaning the emailed link remained valid and potentially usable by an attacker even after being clicked, until the password was completely changed.
root_cause.evidence: The commit message states "When the link is clicked for reset password, the code is exchanged so that the link can not be used again," and the patch modifies the `resetPasswordPage` GET handler in `ResetPasswordController.java` to call `codeStore.retrieveCode(code)` (which deletes it) and generates a new, short-lived (5-minute) code specifically for the form submission.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The password reset link's underlying expiring code was only consumed upon the actual password reset form submission (POST), meaning the emailed link remained valid and potentially usable by an attacker even after being clicked, until the password was completely changed.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The commit message states "When the link is clicked for reset password, the code is exchanged so that the link can not be used again," and the patch modifies the `resetPasswordPage` GET handler in `ResetPasswordController.java` to call `codeStore.retrieveCode(code)` (which deletes it) and generates a new, short-lived (5-minute) code specifically for the form submission.

intent_record.intent_summary: Reorder import statements to conform to stylistic guidelines.
intent_record.what: The import statement for java.sql.Timestamp was moved to follow the org.springframework import.
intent_record.how: This change does not affect the functional behavior of the program at all.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove redundant public modifier from interface method
intent_record.what: Removed the redundant 'public' modifier from the interface method signature.
intent_record.how: This change has no impact on runtime behavior since interface methods in Java are implicitly public.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove redundant public modifiers from interface methods to conform to standard Java style guidelines.
intent_record.what: Removed the 'public' keyword from method declarations in the ExpiringCodeStore interface.
intent_record.how: This change has no runtime effect because methods in Java interfaces are implicitly public.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add imports for ExpiringCode and ExpiringCodeStore to support expiring code functionality.
intent_record.what: Added import statements for ExpiringCode and ExpiringCodeStore.
intent_record.how: Makes the ExpiringCode and ExpiringCodeStore classes available for use within the file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update imports by removing unused Map and adding Timestamp.
intent_record.what: Removed java.util.Map import and added java.sql.Timestamp import.
intent_record.how: Updates the class dependencies to match the actual types used in the file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Inject the ExpiringCodeStore dependency into ResetPasswordController.
intent_record.what: Added an ExpiringCodeStore dependency to the ResetPasswordController class and updated the constructor to initialize it.
intent_record.how: Makes the ExpiringCodeStore instance available for use within the controller's methods.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add validation for the reset password code and regenerate it for the view.
intent_record.what: Updated the method signature to accept request parameters and added logic to validate the provided reset code.
intent_record.how: Checks if the provided code exists; if invalid, it returns an error response, otherwise it generates a new short-lived code and passes it to the view.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove excess whitespace in reset password controller.
intent_record.what: Removed two empty lines inside the try block.
intent_record.how: No functional impact; purely a formatting change.

intent_record.intent_summary: Inject the codeStore dependency into the Spring bean via its constructor.
intent_record.what: Added a new constructor argument referencing 'codeStore' to an existing bean configuration.
intent_record.how: Provides the bean with access to the codeStore dependency upon instantiation, allowing it to utilize code storage functionalities.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add an explicit ID to the UaaExpiringCodeService bean definition to facilitate explicit referencing and dependency injection.
intent_record.what: Added an 'id' attribute with the value 'uaaExpiringCodeService' to the bean definition for UaaExpiringCodeService.
intent_record.how: Allows other components within the Spring application context to explicitly reference and inject this specific bean by its newly assigned ID.

intent_record.intent_summary: Replace direct request parameter access with model attributes to improve security and data flow.
intent_record.what: Replaced direct request parameter references (${param.email[0]} and ${param.code[0]}) with model attributes (${email} and ${code}) in the Thymeleaf template.
intent_record.how: Modifies the template to read the email and code values from the server-provided model attributes rather than directly echoing user-supplied HTTP request parameters, preventing potential injection or XSS issues.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add ExpiringCodeStore import.
intent_record.what: Adds an import statement for ExpiringCodeStore.
intent_record.how: Makes the ExpiringCodeStore class available for use in the file.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add static imports for Mockito's any and anyString matchers.
intent_record.what: Added static imports for org.mockito.Matchers.any and org.mockito.Matchers.anyString.
intent_record.how: Makes the any() and anyString() Mockito matchers available in the local namespace for use in test assertions or stubbing.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add ExpiringCodeStore dependency to the test class to support testing password reset functionality.
intent_record.what: Added a new private field 'codeStore' of type ExpiringCodeStore to the ResetPasswordControllerTest class.
intent_record.how: Provides a reference to the ExpiringCodeStore component within the test environment, enabling tests to interact with or mock expiring codes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update ResetPasswordControllerTest to mock and inject the newly required ExpiringCodeStore dependency.
intent_record.what: Added an ExpiringCodeStore mock and included it in the ResetPasswordController constructor invocation.
intent_record.how: Satisfies the updated constructor signature of ResetPasswordController by providing the newly required ExpiringCodeStore dependency during test setup.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add mock setup for codeStore to ensure the reset password test executes successfully.
intent_record.what: Added mock expectations for codeStore in the testResetPasswordPage method.
intent_record.how: Configures the mock codeStore to return a valid ExpiringCode, allowing the controller to process the reset password request successfully during the test.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix password reset validation by checking the password modification timestamp.
intent_record.what: Extracts passwordModifiedTime from the expiring code data and adds it to the isUserModified method call.
intent_record.how: Validates the reset token against the user's password modification time to prevent using stale or invalidated reset codes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Include and verify the password last modified date to ensure old password reset codes are properly invalidated if the password changes.
intent_record.what: The change updates the PasswordChange constructor to include the user's passwordLastModified date and replaces the generic last-modified timestamp check in isUserModified with a specific check against the passwordLastModified date.
intent_record.how: By embedding the password's last modified date into the expiring code and verifying it during the reset process, the system now accurately invalidates previously generated reset links if the user's password has been changed in the interim.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add passwordModifiedTime to PasswordChange constructor to track modification timestamps.
intent_record.what: Added a 'passwordModifiedTime' Date parameter to the PasswordChange constructor and assigned it to an instance variable.
intent_record.how: Enables the PasswordChange object to store the timestamp of when a password was modified upon instantiation.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add passwordModifiedTime field to the PasswordChange class to track modification timestamps.
intent_record.what: Added a new Date field named passwordModifiedTime with a JSON property annotation.
intent_record.how: Enables the application to store and serialize the timestamp of when a password was modified within the PasswordChange object.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add getter and setter for passwordModifiedTime.
intent_record.what: Added getter and setter methods for the passwordModifiedTime property.
intent_record.how: Allows other components to read and write the passwordModifiedTime field on the PasswordChange object.

intent_record.intent_summary: Optimize timestamp creation by using System.currentTimeMillis() instead of instantiating a temporary Date object.
intent_record.what: Replaced 'new Date().getTime()' with 'System.currentTimeMillis()'.
intent_record.how: Achieves the exact same time value but avoids the unnecessary allocation of a Date object.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add missing import and reorder imports alphabetically.
intent_record.what: Added an import statement for ResetPasswordService and adjusted the position of UaaResetPasswordService.
intent_record.how: Makes the ResetPasswordService interface or class available for use within the file without altering existing runtime behavior.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add a Date variable representing yesterday for use in password reset tests.
intent_record.what: Added a new Date variable initialized to exactly 24 hours prior to the current system time.
intent_record.how: Provides a reusable date instance representing 'yesterday' to be used across the test class methods.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update password reset tests to support the inclusion of a timestamp in the password change payload.
intent_record.what: Modified test mock setups to include a timestamp parameter ('yesterday') in PasswordChange constructors and updated mock return values to use JSON serialized data instead of a simple ID.
intent_record.how: Aligns the test environment with updated application logic that requires a timestamp for password changes, ensuring the mock correctly returns the expected JSON payload.

intent_record.intent_summary: Fix test setup by explicitly setting the password last modified date and refactoring date initialization.
intent_record.what: Simplified date instantiation and added a missing password last modified date to the test user.
intent_record.how: Ensures the test user object is fully populated with necessary state for the password reset test.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove invalid form interactions from the expired password reset link test.
intent_record.what: Removed WebDriver interactions for password input and submit button.
intent_record.how: Prevents the test from attempting to interact with form elements that are not present when an expired reset link is accessed.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update test imports to support new assertions and mock requests.
intent_record.what: Updated imports by adding classes for MockMvc utilities, assertions, and regex, while removing unused imports.
intent_record.how: Provides the necessary dependencies for executing new test methods, assertions, and mock web requests within the test suite.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update PasswordChange instantiation in tests to include the password last modified timestamp.
intent_record.what: Added the getPasswordLastModified() argument to the PasswordChange constructor.
intent_record.how: Updates the test data creation to pass the required password modification timestamp, aligning with the updated PasswordChange object structure.

intent_record.intent_summary: Update PasswordChange instantiation to include passwordLastModified to match the updated class constructor.
intent_record.what: Updated the PasswordChange constructor call to include the user's passwordLastModified timestamp and added spaces around an addition operator.
intent_record.how: Ensures the test correctly initializes the PasswordChange object with the expected arguments, preventing compilation or runtime errors due to API changes.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add tests to verify reset password code generation and expiration behavior.
intent_record.what: Added two new test methods: testResettingAPasswordChangesCodeInForm and testResettingAPasswordFailsWhenPasswordChanged.
intent_record.how: These tests verify that the password reset form generates a new expiring code and that using an old reset code fails if the user's password has already been changed.

intent_record.intent_summary: Remove a timing-dependent test method.
intent_record.what: Removed the testResettingAPasswordUsingTimestampUserModified test method.
intent_record.how: Eliminates a test case that relied on hardcoded thread sleeps to verify password reset behavior after a user entity was modified.

intent_record.intent_summary: Extract method to support creating change password requests with a string code.
intent_record.what: Added an overloaded method that accepts a String for the code parameter instead of an ExpiringCode object.
intent_record.how: Allows creating a change password request using either an ExpiringCode object or a raw String code.

grouping_result.intent: Reorder import statements to conform to stylistic guidelines.
Remove redundant public modifiers from interface methods to conform to standard Java style guidelines.
Inject the codeStore dependency into the Spring bean via its constructor.
Update imports by removing unused Map and adding Timestamp.
Add validation for the reset password code and regenerate it for the view.
Remove excess whitespace in reset password controller.
Add an explicit ID to the UaaExpiringCodeService bean definition to facilitate explicit referencing and dependency injection.
Replace direct request parameter access with model attributes to improve security and data flow.
Update ResetPasswordControllerTest to mock and inject the newly required ExpiringCodeStore dependency.
Update test imports to support new assertions and mock requests.
Add tests to verify reset password code generation and expiration behavior.
Add mock setup for codeStore to ensure the reset password test executes successfully.
Update PasswordChange instantiation to include passwordLastModified to match the updated class constructor.
Include and verify the password last modified date to ensure old password reset codes are properly invalidated if the password changes.
Add getter and setter for passwordModifiedTime.
Optimize timestamp creation by using System.currentTimeMillis() instead of instantiating a temporary Date object.
Add missing import and reorder imports alphabetically.
Remove invalid form interactions from the expired password reset link test.
Remove a timing-dependent test method.
Extract method to support creating change password requests with a string code.

consistency_review.core_intent: Remove redundant public modifiers from interface methods to conform to Java style guidelines.
Add and inject the ExpiringCodeStore dependency into controllers.
Update ResetPasswordControllerTest to include and use the newly required ExpiringCodeStore dependency.
Update test imports to support Mockito matchers and new assertions.
Add tests and necessary test dependencies/variables to verify password reset code generation and expiration behavior.
Update PasswordChange logic and associated tests to properly include and validate the password last modified timestamp.
Add the passwordModifiedTime field, constructor parameter, and getter/setter to the PasswordChange class.

relevance_review.reason: The group only contains stylistic changes to import statements, which do not affect the logic of the password reset mechanism or address the vulnerability.
The group only contains style changes (removing redundant public modifiers from interface methods), which are completely unrelated to the vulnerability involving password reset token consumption.

Injecting the ExpiringCodeStore dependency into the ResetPasswordController provides the necessary components to check and consume the expiring code earlier in the process (e.g., during the GET request), directly supporting the fix for the vulnerability.
Updating imports by removing unused classes and adding new ones is a standard code maintenance task and does not address the logic flaw regarding the consumption of password reset codes.

The group adds validation and regenerates the reset password code for the view, which addresses the root cause of the emailed link remaining valid by ensuring the code is consumed or regenerated prior to the final POST request.
The group only removes excess whitespace, which is a formatting change and does not address the logic of when the password reset token is consumed.
Adding an explicit bean ID for dependency injection is a configuration change and does not directly address the logic of when the expiring code is consumed.

The group's core intent is to replace direct request parameter access with model attributes, which is a general refactoring or web framework usage improvement and does not address the logic of when a password reset code is consumed.

The group updates the ResetPasswordControllerTest to include the ExpiringCodeStore dependency, which is the mechanism introduced to correctly manage and consume expiring codes for password resets, directly addressing the root cause.
The group only updates test imports for Mockito matchers and assertions, which is test refactoring and does not directly address the logic of consuming the password reset link's expiring code.
The group adds tests specifically designed to verify the generation and expiration behavior of password reset codes, which directly addresses the vulnerability by ensuring the fix works as intended.
The group adds test mock setup for the codeStore, which is directly related to testing the fix for the expiring code consumption logic during password resets.

The group modifies password reset validation logic by incorporating a password last modified timestamp, which is directly related to addressing how password reset tokens are validated and expired, aligning with the vulnerability root cause.
The group modifies the token validation logic for password reset codes, which is directly related to addressing the vulnerability involving the lifecycle and expiration of password reset links.

Adding a 'passwordModifiedTime' field to track when a password was last changed is likely part of the mechanism to invalidate expiring codes or manage the lifecycle of password reset links, directly addressing the root cause.
The group only contains a minor performance optimization for timestamp creation, which has no bearing on the logic for consuming password reset codes.
The group only contains import reordering and additions, which are standard code maintenance tasks unrelated to the password reset token expiration logic.
The group updates tests specifically for expired password reset links, which directly relates to the vulnerability involving the timing of reset link expiration and consumption.
The group only removes a timing-dependent test method, which is unrelated to the vulnerability involving the password reset code consumption.

The group only involves extracting a method to support creating change password requests, which is a refactoring change and does not directly address the vulnerability's root cause of consuming the reset token upon clicking the link.

candidates.rationale: The GET method for the password reset page does not validate or consume the reset code, leaving it valid until the POST request is submitted.
The endpoint simply returns the view without validating the expiring code or checking its status, leading to the vulnerability.

vuln_statements.explanation: This method handles the GET request for the password reset page but fails to validate or consume the reset code, which is the root cause of the vulnerability.
This statement simply returns the view without validating the expiring code or checking its status.

[skip ci]
Initial merger of Login Server and UAA
What remains is swap all controllers in the Login Server to use beans i
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 327fbdc2c2f8595b1fbc09f5925c907b42f2d45a
Author: Filip Hanik <fhanik@pivotal.io>
Date:   Thu Nov 6 18:36:57 2014 -0700

    [skip ci]
    Initial merger of Login Server and UAA
    What remains is swap all controllers in the Login Server to use beans instead of rest templates
    
    [skip ci]

diff --git a/login/src/main/java/org/cloudfoundry/identity/uaa/login/ResetPasswordController.java b/login/src/main/java/org/cloudfoundry/identity/uaa/login/ResetPasswordController.java
new file mo
bic_trace_step.reason: The 'resetPasswordPage' method handling the GET request to '/reset_password' simply returns the 'reset_password' view without validating or consuming the expiring code. The code is only passed to 'resetPasswordService.resetPassword(code, password)' during the POST request in 'resetPassword.do'. This matches the root cause where the code is only consumed upon form submission.

Author: Filip Hanik <fhanik@pivotal.io>

    [skip ci]
    Initial merger of Login Server and UAA

diff --git a/login/src/main/java/org/cloudfoundry/identity/uaa/login/ResetPasswordController.java b/login/src/main/java/org/cloudfoundry/identity/uaa/login/ResetPasswordController.java
new file mo
bic_trace_step.reason: The GET handler for '/reset_password' (resetPasswordPage) simply returns the 'reset_password' view without consuming or validating the 'code'. The 'code' is only processed and consumed during the POST request to '/reset_password.do' via resetPasswordService.resetPassword(). This matches the vulnerability root cause where the code remains valid even after the link is clicked, until the form is submitted.
